LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IRELAND SAN FILIPPO, L.L.P.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES IT SUFFERED A LOSS SEPARATE AND APART FROM ITS MEMBERS AND ITS MEMBERS HAVE ASSIGNED THEIR CLAIMS TO PLAINTIFF. ATLAS HOTEL IS DISTINGUISHABLE FROM THIS CASE FOR IT INVOLVED AN ACTION AGAINST THE INTERNAL REVENUE AND THE FEDERAL ASSIGNMENT OF CLAIMS ACT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED AT THIS EARLY STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. CAMENISHCH V SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 44 CAL. APP. 4TH 1689, 1699. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Janet Everson of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (788.1900) for defendant and moving party
Denis Rice of Howard Rice (434.1600) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT IRELAND SAN FILIPPO, LLP'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT is denied without prejudice because counsel's declaration fails to demonstrate why it would be in the interest of justice to grant leave and an explanation as to why it was not submitted at an earlier date. CCP 426.50; The Rutter Guide Civil Procedure Before Trial 6.562. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

ATLAS HOTEL IS DISTINGUISHABLE FROM THIS CASE FOR IT INVOLVED AN ACTION AGAINST THE INTERNAL REVENUE AND THE FEDERAL ASSIGNMENT OF CLAIMS ACT. THE MOTION TO STRIKE ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED AT THIS EARLY STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. CAMENISHCH V SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 44 CAL. APP. 4TH 1689, 1699. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
